focus: Final Review + Advancement Exam Preparation — All L2 Accent Skills
focus_ar: المراجعة النهائية + التحضير لامتحان الترقية — كل مهارات النطق في المستوى الثاني
arabic_challenge: You've spent 11 weeks building on your L1 foundation: native-speed L1 review (Wk1), reduction/weak forms (Wk2), yod-dropping (Wk3), emphasis/contrast intonation (Wk4), dark L (Wk5), question/list/tag intonation (Wk6), NG-coalescence (Wk7), thought groups (Wk8), emphatic argumentation (Wk9), advanced tag nuance (Wk10), and native-speed shadowing/transcription (Wk11). This final week mirrors L1's own Week 10 structure: no new material, just rigorous, honest review and mock-exam preparation for the L2→L3 advancement exam.

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review Weeks 1-3: native-speed L1 integration, reduction/weak forms, yod-dropping

isolation.instructions: Revisit your Week 1-3 recordings before today's drill, noting how much more automatic these advanced patterns feel now.
isolation.instructions_ar: راجع تسجيلات الأسبوع ١-٣ قبل تمرين النهارده، ولاحظ قد إيه الأنماط المتقدمة دي بقت تلقائية أكتر دلوقتي.
practice: fish and chips, could have gone, new/tune/duke — at natural speed

sentence_practice: I could have gone for fish and chips, but I chose a new tune instead.
The new duke should have tuned his approach to the news.
Fish and chips, a new tune, and I could have gone earlier.
daily_drills.record_this: I could have gone for fish and chips, but I chose a new tune instead — the new duke should have tuned his approach to the news.
daily_drills.self_check: Compare today's fluency to your actual Week 1-3 recordings. Is the improvement clear?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review Weeks 4-6: emphasis/contrast, dark L, question/list/tag intonation

isolation.instructions: Revisit Week 4-6 patterns — contrastive emphasis, dark L at syllable-end, and the question/list/tag intonation system.
isolation.instructions_ar: راجع أنماط الأسبوع ٤-٦ — النبر التناقضي، الـ L الغامقة، ونظام تنغيم الأسئلة والقوائم والأسئلة الذيلية.
practice: I said BLUE, not green. Feel cool, political, global. Are you coming? What time is it?

sentence_practice: I said BLUE, not green — is this political and global issue clear to you?
Are you coming? What time is it, and what's the political impact globally?
This is a global, political issue — I said BLUE, not green, isn't it clear?
daily_drills.record_this: I said BLUE, not green — is this political and global issue clear to you? Are you coming, and what time is it?
daily_drills.self_check: Are contrastive emphasis, dark L, and the question/tag intonation patterns all still distinct and correct after the review gap?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review Weeks 7-9: NG-coalescence, thought groups, emphatic argumentation

isolation.instructions: Revisit Week 7-9 patterns — the merged NG sound, strategic pausing for thought groups, and building persuasive emphasis across an argument.
isolation.instructions_ar: راجع أنماط الأسبوع ٧-٩ — صوت NG المدمج، التوقف الاستراتيجي لمجموعات الفكر، وبناء تأكيد إقناعي عبر الحجة.
practice: think, banking, finger — After a long day, | I like to relax. — The EVIDENCE is clear.

pair: After a long day, | I like to relax. (thought group)

sentence_practice: I think the EVIDENCE, | after a long day of banking research, | is clear.
My finger points to the EVIDENCE — | this REALLY matters.
Thinking about banking, | the EVIDENCE shows real, | lasting change.
daily_drills.record_this: I think the EVIDENCE, after a long day of banking research, is clear — my finger points to it, and this REALLY matters.
daily_drills.self_check: Did NG-coalescence, thought-group pausing, and emphatic argumentation all combine naturally in this review passage?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review Weeks 10-11: advanced tag-question nuance, native-speed shadowing/transcription

isolation.instructions: Revisit Week 10-11 patterns — the finer social nuances of tag questions, and your listen-transcribe-shadow skill for natural reduced speech.
isolation.instructions_ar: راجع أنماط الأسبوع ١٠-١١ — الفروق الاجتماعية الدقيقة في الأسئلة الذيلية، ومهارة الاستماع-التفريغ-المحاكاة للكلام المخفف الطبيعي.
practice: Nice weather, isn't it? (confident) — You haven't seen my keys, have you? (genuine) — I shoulda called her.

sentence_practice: Nice weather, isn't it? You haven't seen my keys, have you?
I shoulda called her — didja hear the news?
This is gonna be great, isn't it? I shoulda known that already.
daily_drills.record_this: Nice weather, isn't it? You haven't seen my keys, have you? I shoulda called her — this is gonna be great, isn't it?
daily_drills.self_check: Could you correctly distinguish tag-question tones AND naturally produce reduced forms in the same passage?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Full mock advancement-exam accent section (read passage + spontaneous debate)

isolation.instructions: Simulate the real L2→L3 advancement exam Accent and Speaking sections: read a passage aloud, then deliver a spontaneous 60-90 second persuasive argument, per blueprint Table 45.
isolation.instructions_ar: حاكي قسمي النطق والكلام في امتحان الترقية L2→L3 الحقيقي: اقرأ فقرة بصوت عالي، وبعدين قدم حجة إقناعية عفوية لمدة ٦٠-٩٠ ثانية.
practice: Part A: read the Day 6 review passage below aloud.
Part B: deliver a spontaneous persuasive argument on a topic of your choice for the L2→L3 exam's speaking section.

sentence_practice: Part A — read aloud: 'Not only is this a global issue, but it's also deeply political — the EVIDENCE, having been reviewed by experts, is clear.'
Part B — speak freely: 'Argue for or against a position of your choice for 60-90 seconds, using this level's accent and argumentation skills.'
daily_drills.record_this: (Record both Part A — reading — and Part B — spontaneous persuasive speech — as two separate clips.)
daily_drills.self_check: Compare your Part A (read) fluency to your Part B (spontaneous) fluency. How close is the gap now, compared to your L1 Week 10 mock exam?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Comprehensive Review: all 11 L2 accent features combined
challenge_sentences: Not only is this a global, political issue, but the EVIDENCE, having been reviewed carefully, is also clear — didja think about the new banking policy?
I shoulda known this feels political and global — after a long day of thinking, | the EVIDENCE REALLY matters, isn't it obvious?
Having studied the data, | I think the finger of blame points to policy, | not people — this REALLY matters, doesn't it?
daily_drills.record_this: Not only is this a global, political issue, but the EVIDENCE, having been reviewed carefully, is also clear — after a long day of thinking, this REALLY matters, isn't it obvious?
daily_drills.self_check: This is the longest, most demanding passage in all of L2. Score yourself using L1's Day-7 scoring bands across all 11 features combined.

daily_drills.target_sounds: Final Self-Assessment — L2 Accent Completion Checkpoint

test_yourself.instructions: This is your official L2 accent completion checkpoint. Record yourself reading the passage below, then record 60-90 seconds of spontaneous persuasive speech about your Level 2 journey overall.
test_yourself.passage: Not only have I grown as a speaker over these twelve weeks, but I've also learned to think and argue with real confidence in English. Having studied reduction, dark L, and even the tricky NG sound, I feel prepared for whatever comes next. Rarely have I worked this hard on anything, and the EVIDENCE — my own recordings from week one compared to now — REALLY shows it. This is gonna be a great foundation for Level 3, isn't it?

sounds_to_check.dark L (Wk5): feel, political (implied), REALLY (implied context), foundation, Level
sounds_to_check.inversion (Wk12 grammar, spoken naturall: Not only have I grown..., Rarely have I worked...

scoring_guide.90_100: All 11 L2 features present and mostly automatic in both the read passage and spontaneous speech. Ready for the L2→L3 advancement exam's accent and speaking sections.
scoring_guide.70_89: Most features present in the read passage; some inconsistency in spontaneous speech. Continue practicing before the exam.
scoring_guide.50_69: Features present individually but not yet fully integrated — recommend repeating Week 9's emphasis integration and Week 11's shadowing drills before attempting the exam.
scoring_guide.below_50: Significant gaps remain — recommend repeating the specific weak weeks (identify from Day 1-4 reviews) before requesting the advancement exam.

for_arabic_speakers: This week deliberately mirrors L1's Week 10 structure (self-assessment + mock exam) — by now this format should feel like a familiar, trusted checkpoint ritual.
The Day 5 mock exam previews the real L2→L3 advancement exam format from blueprint Table 45 — Live Speaking (debate/persuasion) and Accent Evaluation sections specifically.

You have now completed the full blueprint-assigned L2 phoneme/connected-speech curriculum (Table 19's yod-dropping, dark L, NG-coalescence, plus §5.3's reduction and §5.4's intonation) — Level 3 will focus on refinement and native-like flow rather than new mechanics.
supplementary_notes.daily_time: 25-30 minutes this week (review + mock exam recording)
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record Day 5 (mock exam) and Day 7 (final assessment) and keep both — compare them against your L1 Week 10 recordings to see your full L1-to-L2 growth arc.
